author: Dr. David Park (PGY-1)
content:
INTERNAL MEDICINE PROGRESS NOTE — HOSPITAL DAY 8
Date/Time: 3/22/2027, 8:30 AM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Team: Dr. Sarah Beckman (attending), Dr. Maria Castro (PGY-3), Dr. David Park (PGY-1)

SUBJECTIVE:
Patient reports she 'feels a little better today.' Slept ~6 hours overnight. Tolerated NG feeds overnight without intolerance. Mother reports her face looks 'less puffy.' Continues to express low mood. Smiled when her younger brother visited yesterday.

Denied: chest pain, dyspnea worse than baseline, fever, severe headache, focal weakness, vision changes, abdominal pain, vomiting, melena, hematuria.

VITALS (24h):
Tmax 99.4 F, Tcurrent 98.0 F
BP range 96-108 / 56-66
HR range 88-104
RR 16-20
SpO2 96-99% on room air (off O2 since HD3)
Weight AM: 37.8 kg (up 2.4 kg from admission)
I/O 24h: in 2480 mL / out 1810 mL

FOCUSED EXAM:
General: Cachetic but appears improved from HD1. Better color.
Neuro: Awake, alert, oriented x 3. Voice still nasal.
MSK: Hip flexion 3/5 R/L (up from 2/5). Shoulder abduction 3/5 bilat. Neck flexion 3-/5. Knee extension 4-/5. Distal 4+/5. Sitting balance: sits edge of bed with 2-person assist for 5 min.
Skin: Scaling dermatosis appears to be peaking with continued desquamation; underlying skin less inflamed.
Abdomen: Less distended; mild residual flank edema; bowel sounds normoactive.
Extremities: Pitting edema reduced from +2 to +1 at ankles.
Lungs: Clear; small bibasilar dullness (persistent improving pleural effusions).
Oropharynx: 3 of 4 oral ulcers healing.

NEW RESULTS (past 48h):
- Muscle biopsy of right vastus lateralis (3/19, finalized 3/22 AM): see NOTE017 for pathology.
- Myositis-specific antibody panel: ALL NEGATIVE (Jo-1, MDA5, TIF1g, Mi-2, NXP2, SAE, PM-Scl, Ku, Ro, La, U1-RNP).
- Skin biopsy (3/20, finalized 3/22 AM): see NOTE018 for dermatopathology.
- Pre-immunosuppression infection screen (HD2-3): all negative.
- 24-hour urine: 0.62 g protein/d; calculated CrCl 85 mL/min.
- Echo (HD2): LVEF 60%, mild Grade I diastolic dysfunction, trace pericardial effusion (no tamponade), no vegetations.
- MBSS (HD3): severe oropharyngeal dysphagia with thin-liquid aspiration. NPO with NG.
- EMG/NCS (HD4): active inflammatory myopathy pattern. No neuropathy. No NMJ disorder.

DAILY LABS (3/22 AM):
Hgb 8.2 (nadir 7.4), WBC 2.85 (nadir 2.10), Plt 138 (nadir 110). Pancytopenia improving.
Albumin 1.9 (up from admission 1.6). Prealbumin 9 (up from 6).
K 4.0, Mg 1.9, Phos 2.8 — all in range on advancing feeds.
CK 1820 (down from peak 2580 HD3). Aldolase 18. LDH 502.
Anti-dsDNA 38 (down from 52). C3 68 (up from 56), C4 6 (up from 4).
UPC 0.45 (improving from 0.61).
TSH 4.62, free T4 0.9 (on levothyroxine 50 mcg).
LFTs: AST 78 (down from 142), ALT 52 (down from 86).

ASSESSMENT / PLAN BY PROBLEM:

1. Active SLE with inflammatory myositis (biopsy w/ PM/DM overlap features, MSA negative):
   - With biopsy back, MSA negative, and infection screen clean, escalating to PULSE methylprednisolone 1000 mg IV daily x 3 days (HD8-10, starting today).
   - Continue HCQ 200 BID via NG.
   - MMF restarted 3/19 at 500 mg BID; advanced to 1 g BID 3/21.
   - Will reassess after pulse, transition back to prednisone 1 mg/kg/day on HD11.

2. Oropharyngeal dysphagia (per MBSS):
   - NG in place. NPO. Aspiration precautions ongoing.
   - Repeat MBSS planned HD12.
   - PT/OT consult ordered for bedside-to-chair work.

3. Protein-calorie malnutrition / secondary nutrition deficits:
   - Continue nutrition plan (per Eun-Ji Lee, RD). Currently Nutren 1.0 at 35 mL/hr (840 kcal/d). Plan to advance today.
   - Continue thiamine, multivit, folate, zinc, vitamin D.
   - Continue Q6h electrolyte monitoring x 24-48h more, then daily.

4. Pancytopenia: improving with treatment. No transfusion required. Continue daily CBC.

5. Lupus nephritis (mild proteinuria UPC 0.62 -> 0.45):
   - Trending down. Renal biopsy deferred pending stabilization.

6. Hashimoto's thyroiditis: levothyroxine 50 mcg daily (increased from 25 on 3/16).

7. ID prophylaxis: TMP/SMX M/W/F (PCP prophylaxis); fluconazole 200 mg PO daily during pulse + 7 days.

8. DVT prophylaxis: mechanical only (platelets 138). Reconsider chemo prophylaxis if plt >150 stable.

9. Psych CL involvement; Spanish-speaking outpatient therapy continued post-discharge.

10. Social work / case management: third Medicaid application filed with legal aid. 90-day post-discharge emergency Medicaid confirmed. Discharge planning underway.

Family meeting planned 4 PM today with patient, mother, aunt, social work, attending, rheum, nutrition.

Documented at bedside in real time.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. David Park, MD (PGY-1) — primary author
Reviewed and co-signed by:
Dr. Maria Castro, MD (PGY-3)
Dr. Sarah Beckman, MD (Attending)
Date: 3/22/2027
